behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive error messages.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser validation/error construction in a single component. No real OS/network/subprocess dependency, and no multi-module handoff is required to reproduce invalid-token/range failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser directly with table-driven invalid inputs (`13 * * * *`, `* * 32 * *`, malformed token counts/symbols) and assert structured error code/message includes the offending field/value.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday cases.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is date/time calculation logic for cron semantics; it can be exercised as a pure function from parsed schedule + start time to next N timestamps. No real infra or LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke next-run calculator with prebuilt schedule objects and fixed UTC start times; assert exact next N timestamps for cases like `L` (month end across 28/29/30/31-day months) and `W` (nearest weekday when date lands on weekend).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules that fire within configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears at module handoff: schedule expansion/next-fire generation feeding overlap logic. Unit-testing overlap math alone can miss boundary bugs in timestamp normalization/order between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + next-run generator + overlap detector together in-process. For two cron inputs and a 5-minute gap, generate upcoming windows and assert detected conflict intervals match expected timestamps (including near-boundary 4m59s vs 5m00s cases).

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in interaction between scheduler logic and timezone/DST rules (zone conversion, ambiguous/nonexistent local times). No subprocess/network required, but mocking timezone behavior would hide real DST edge failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process with real timezone data (e.g., `America/New_York`): compute next runs around DST start and end for `30 2 * * *`; assert documented policy for nonexistent 2:30 in spring and duplicated 2:30 in fall is applied exactly.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is in the past, engine skips or backfills according to missed-run policy.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on combining schedule computation, current-time comparison, and policy evaluation. Single-function tests can miss bugs in state handoff (computed time semantics vs policy decision). No real external infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise scheduler service with real calculator + policy module using a frozen clock. Provide jobs whose next run is before `now` and assert resulting action (`skip`, `enqueue backfill`, possibly count-limited backfills) matches policy configuration.
